title: Tell HMRC about a change to your company benefits
body: " Give us feedback.  We need your help to improve and to make sure GOV.UK works well for you. You can give us feedback about this guidance to help make GOV.UK better.   You pay tax on company benefits like cars, medical insurance and living accommodation.  You’ll pay more tax and your take home pay will reduce if:   you start getting a company benefit  the value of your company benefit increases   You’ll pay less tax and your take home pay will increase if:   you stop getting a company benefit the value of your company benefit decreases   Find out more about tax on company benefits and the types of benefits that are taxable.  Check your details online   You can use the PAYE section of HMRC online services or the HMRC app to check:    all your company benefits are shown in your PAYE Income Tax summary the value of each company benefit is correct in your tax-free amount calculation   Using the HMRC online service:   Sign in to HMRC online services. Go to the ‘Pay As You Earn (PAYE)’ section. Select ‘check current tax year’ to view your PAYE Income Tax Summary for 6 April to 5 April. Select ‘view or update company benefits’.   Using the HMRC app   Download the HMRC app. Go to the ‘Pay As You Earn (PAYE)’ section. Select ‘what is a tax-free amount?’.   Update your details if they are wrong or missing  If your details are wrong or missing you can use the HMRC online services or the HMRC app  to:   add a missing company benefit tell us the amount of company benefit has changed tell us the start and end dates of your company benefits   If you have other details to update you can tell HMRC about:   starting a new job having more than one job a change in your employment income  a change to your pension income    Keeping your tax code up to date helps to make sure you pay the correct amount of tax.  This makes it less likely you will pay too much or too little tax.  After you update your details  Not all changes you tell HMRC about will affect your tax code. We will tell your employer or pension provider if your tax code changes. Your next payslip should show your new tax code and any adjustments if you were paying the wrong amount of tax.   When you sign up for paperless updates you will get a message if your tax code changes. You can view these updates in the:   messages section of your HMRC online account communication section of your HMRC app   Go paperless for tax code updates  You can choose to receive updates about any changes to your tax code by email or as an online message in your HMRC online account or HMRC app. Find out how to go paperless for tax code updates.  If you need extra help using HMRC online services  You can find out how to:    get help from HMRC if you need extra support if you cannot...
